danger_signals.visual_form: Alien creatures and possessed soldiers in varied sprite forms across stages
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat; player must engage or avoid

danger_signals.visual_form: Large distinctive sprite sprites at stage endpoints; flashing red when near defeat (unused but programmed)
Military base structures, jungle terrain, alien lair interior layouts with obstacles
Human soldier sprites that behave as enemies
danger_signals.meaning: Moral/tactical danger; former allies now hostile

danger_signals.visual_form: Stationary turret sprites with pointing behavior (can face left or right directionally)

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying item capsules containing actual gun sprites (machine gun, spread gun, bomb gun, laser gun)
safety_signals.meaning: Player resource increase; temporary safety advantage

safety_signals.visual_form: Visual transition from one environment to next (base exterior → interior → jungle → alien lair)
Medal sprites representing Bill and Lance (EU Probotector version shows miniature robot protagonists)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Military commando sprites; EU version: robot soldiers RD008 and RC011
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: In top-down stages, player sprites differ from side-scrolling proportions to match perspective
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base exteriors and interiors; command presence throughout stages

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Generic alien creature sprites; mutated forms distinct from original Contra invaders
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard aliens appear across all stages; boss serpent on level 6 has unused path variants (4 paths, only 2 used)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Alien lair (stages 5-6); possessed military base; jungle corrupted by alien presence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Human soldier sprites repurposed as hostile NPCs; visually identical to commando player characters but behavior is enemy
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Greeders (level 7 and others) have unused ducking behavior to hide behind sandbags
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Military base interiors; areas showing signs of occupation/control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military base exteriors show strategic positioning; alien structures begin appearing in stage backdrops
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol and attack aggressively; possessed soldiers behave erratically

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage 1 shows exterior defense perimeter; Stage 2 (top-down) reveals base interior complexity; infrastructure appears damaged
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mixed human and alien enemies throughout; turrets defend specific positions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stage 3 shifts environment to jungle terrain; alien presence integrated into natural setting
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy spawns change to reflect jungle-adapted aliens; different obstacle types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Stages 4-5 feature increasingly alien architecture; bio-organic structures replace mechanical elements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss encounters intensify; enemy density increases; final stage shows alien nest interior (NES exclusive)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Same stages replay after credits; no visual distinction between loop 1 and loop 2
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Difficulty increases to maximum; no continues available; weapons/hyper shells carry over

environmental_storytelling.example: Five stages trace invasion: military base exterior → base interior (top-down) → jungle → alien lair exterior → alien lair interior, showing escalation from defensive position to enemy stronghold
Human soldiers appear as enemies within military structures, indicating Red Falcon control has reached command personnel
Flying capsules containing actual gun sprites (not abstract icons like original Contra) emphasize materiality of arms escalation
Top-down stages (2, 5) show interior complexity and scale of base/alien lair, contrasting with side-scrolling exterior perspectives
EU Probotector II replaces human commando violence with robot protagonists and human enemies with robot variants, fundamentally altering the war narrative (human soldiers → combat robots)

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard running/shooting patterns; unused ducking behavior exists (hiding behind sandbags in areas 1 and 3) showing defensive adaptation capability
Can point left or right; normally only approach from left, but rightward-pointing capability programmed but unused
Flashing red when health is critical; functionality programmed but never triggered in final game
4 movement paths coded; only 2 used in actual gameplay, indicating design simplification or unused difficulty variation
Jump height now controllable by holding joystick up/down while pressing jump (new to Super Contra); indicates increased player agency in vertical terrain

notes: Super C shows world-state primarily through environment transitions (base→jungle→alien lair) rather than dynamic visual corruption. The EU Probotector II regional variant fundamentally alters faction identity by removing human combatants entirely, converting possessed soldiers and protagonists to robots—a political/cultural censorship decision that changes the game's core narrative of humans fighting aliens to robots fighting aliens. Multiple unused behavioral systems (Greeder ducking, turret directionality, boss flashing) suggest more complex enemy AI was prototyped. The arcade version's second-loop mechanic creates world-state through difficulty escalation rather than visual change.
